groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Announcement subject, type, goal, audience, facts, language, timing, action, destination, and community context.

groups.title: Message & Delivery
groups.description: Discord publishing mode, tone, target length, mention behavior, and channel-specific context.

groups.title: Brand & Formatting
groups.description: Community voice, emoji level, Discord formatting style, and optional short-version output.

groups.title: Accuracy & Restrictions
groups.description: Verified claims, disclosures, privacy restrictions, factual boundaries, and uncommon requirements.

variables.label: Announcement subject
variables.placeholder: New feature release, scheduled maintenance, community event...
variables.hint: State the main subject in one clear phrase.

help.what: Provide the announcement subject for this Discord announcement.
help.why: It establishes the central topic around which the announcement is written.
help.example: Good example: Scheduled maintenance for the creator dashboard
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing announcements, or changing the topic into a different update.

variables.label: Announcement type
variables.placeholder: Select an announcement type
variables.hint: Helps organize the message around the correct Discord use case.

help.what: Provide the announcement type for this Discord announcement.
help.why: Different announcement types require different priorities, details, and levels of urgency.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing announcements, or changing the topic into a different update.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Choose the main outcome
variables.hint: Select the single most important result the announcement should achieve.

help.what: Provide the primary goal for this Discord announcement.
help.why: It determines what information receives the strongest emphasis.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing announcements, or changing the topic into a different update.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: All members, new users, moderators, paid members, developers...
variables.hint: Describe who should read and act on the announcement.

help.what: Provide the target audience for this Discord announcement.
help.why: The model cannot safely infer which server members the message is intended for.
help.example: Good example: All active Creatornivo community members
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing announcements, or changing the topic into a different update.

variables.label: Essential details
variables.placeholder: Add the confirmed information members need to know.
variables.hint: Include the update, impact, dates, requirements, benefits, or changes.

help.what: Provide the essential details for this Discord announcement.
help.why: It supplies the factual content that cannot be invented or inferred.
help.example: Good example: Maintenance runs July 18, 2026 from 02:00 to 04:00 UTC. Generation and library access may be unavailable during that window.
help.avoid: Avoid assumptions, fake urgency, invented dates, unsupported features, guessed causes, fake links, or vague filler.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: English, Spanish, Ukrainian, German...
variables.hint: The complete generated package will use this language.

help.what: Provide the output language for this Discord announcement.
help.why: Discord communities may publish in any language, so the correct language cannot always be inferred.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing announcements, or changing the topic into a different update.

variables.label: Server or community context
variables.placeholder: Gaming clan, SaaS community, creator server, customer support...
variables.hint: Briefly describe the community so the message sounds appropriate.

help.what: Optional context for the server or community context field.
help.why: Community context materially affects vocabulary, assumed knowledge, and formality.
help.example: Good example: SaaS product community for creators using AI content workflows.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Date, time, and timezone
variables.placeholder: July 18, 2026 at 19:00 UTC, maintenance from 02:00-04:00...
variables.hint: Include exact scheduling details and a timezone when relevant.

help.what: Optional context for the date, time, and timezone field.
help.why: Events, maintenance, releases, and deadlines require exact user-supplied timing.
help.avoid: Avoid vague relative timing without a date, missing time zones, guessed deadlines, or unverified Discord timestamps.

variables.label: Primary link
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/event
variables.hint: Add the main destination only; the generator will not invent links.

help.what: Optional context for the primary link field.
help.why: The correct destination URL must come directly from the user.
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, private, misleading, or unsupported links.

variables.label: Desired member action
variables.placeholder: Register, read the new rules, update the app, join the event...
variables.hint: Leave blank when the announcement is purely informational.

help.what: Optional context for the desired member action field.
help.why: It defines the CTA without forcing one when no action is required.
help.example: Good example: Check the status page before reporting duplicate issues.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Publishing mode
variables.placeholder: Select how the announcement will be posted
variables.hint: Choose a regular message, crosspost, or bot-ready embed.

help.what: Optional context for the publishing mode field.
help.why: Standard messages, crossposts, and embeds require different structures and mention handling.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select a tone
variables.hint: Choose the overall delivery style without changing the underlying facts.

help.what: Optional context for the tone field.
help.why: Discord server cultures vary substantially in tone and level of formality.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Message length
variables.placeholder: Select a target length
variables.hint: Auto chooses the shortest length that communicates the full update.

help.what: Optional context for the message length field.
help.why: A brief event reminder and a detailed policy change require different content depth.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Mention preference
variables.placeholder: Choose how mentions should be handled
variables.hint: The generator will never invent a role or user mention.

help.what: Optional context for the mention preference field.
help.why: Discord mentions affect notifications and must be selected deliberately.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported mass mentions, fake role names, invented user IDs, repeated pings, or engagement bait.

variables.label: Channel context
variables.placeholder: #announcements, #events, #status-updates, read-only channel...
variables.hint: Describe where the message will appear or how the channel is used.

help.what: Optional context for the channel context field.
help.why: Channel context helps prevent redundant wording and improves placement guidance.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Brand or community voice
variables.placeholder: Friendly and direct, developer-focused, playful but not childish...
variables.hint: Add voice characteristics or a short style example.

help.what: Optional context for the brand or community voice field.
help.why: It lets established communities preserve a recognizable communication style.
help.example: Good example: Clear, practical, transparent, and support-focused.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Emoji use
variables.placeholder: Select an emoji level
variables.hint: Controls visual emphasis without changing the message structure.

help.what: Optional context for the emoji use field.
help.why: Emoji expectations differ significantly between professional and informal servers.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Formatting style
variables.placeholder: Select a Discord formatting style
variables.hint: Controls paragraph, heading, and bullet use.

help.what: Optional context for the formatting style field.
help.why: The same information may need a conversational post or a structured operational update.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Include short version
variables.placeholder: Choose whether to include a short version
variables.hint: Adds a compact alternative for reminders or secondary channels.

help.what: Optional context for the include short version field.
help.why: A reusable reminder version is particularly useful for Discord announcements.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.

variables.label: Verified facts or proof
variables.placeholder: Confirmed figures, approved quotations, release status, validated results...
variables.hint: Add exact claims that are approved for publication.

help.what: Optional context for the verified facts or proof field.
help.why: It separates approved evidence from unsupported promotional assumptions.
help.example: Good example: Maintenance window confirmed by the engineering team. No customer data changes are planned.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported statistics, quotes, reviews, proof, member counts, results, approvals, or partnerships.

variables.label: Required disclosure
variables.placeholder: Sponsored event, affiliate relationship, staff affiliation, contest terms...
variables.hint: Include any relationship or commercial context members should know.

help.what: Optional context for the required disclosure field.
help.why: Community promotions and partnerships may require transparent affiliation disclosure.
help.example: Good example: Staff announcement from the Creatornivo team.
help.avoid: Avoid hiding sponsorships, affiliate relationships, staff affiliation, paid promotions, contest terms, or commercial context.

variables.label: Restrictions and must-avoid items
variables.placeholder: Do not mention pricing, avoid specific wording, protect member identities...
variables.hint: List prohibited claims, sensitive details, or mandatory limitations.

help.what: Optional context for the restrictions and must-avoid items field.
help.why: It consolidates factual, privacy, legal, and brand restrictions into one field.
help.example: Good example: Do not imply data loss, security impact, or guaranteed completion time.
help.avoid: Avoid burying privacy, legal, factual, moderation, or brand limits in another field.

variables.label: Additional context
variables.placeholder: Add any relevant information not covered above.
variables.hint: Use only for context that materially affects the announcement.

help.what: Optional context for the additional context field.
help.why: It provides one controlled place for uncommon requirements without expanding the main form.
help.example: Good example: Keep the message easy to scan on mobile and avoid fake urgency.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported social proof, private data, repeated CTAs, or unnecessary mass mentions.
